## Thread: Bubble Sort — 6 msgs

_Topics: [`VSAM, files, sorting`](topics.md#files)_

### Bubble Sort

- **From:** John DeWitt <kadet@wworx.net>
- **Date:** 1999-03-24T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<36F9A9A2.AF91C68F@wworx.net>`

```
Help!  Does anyone have a method of setting up a "bubble sort" type of
array to strip off top 1000 rec of a 20000 rec DB2 table?  Need the
amounts but the table is indexed differently, so currently I have to get
all 20000, write to a sequential file, sort, then send top 1000 to the
report generator.  Any help?
John DeWitt
kadet@wworx.net
```

#### ↳ Re: Bubble Sort

- **From:** "Judson McClendon" <judmc123@bellsouth.net>
- **Date:** 1999-03-25T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<NgsK2.18149$Ek.15628906@news1.mia>`
- **References:** `<36F9A9A2.AF91C68F@wworx.net>`

```
John DeWitt wrote:
>
>Does anyone know how to set up a "bubble sort"  that will allow me to
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
>write out only those top 1000 recs (save CPU and space).  Any ideas?

Why not use the SORT verb?  But if you can't or won't use SORT, there
is a much faster way to do what you want, and it takes much less
storage space as well.

1. Define a table of size N = number of records you want to select.
   In your case N = 1000.  As you insert records into the table you
   will maintain the table in reverse order: table(i) >= table(i + 1).

2. Making one pass of the file.  For each record read if the amount is
   greater than the lowest amount in the stored table, insert the new
   record into the table using insertion sort.  When the table becomes
   full, the lowest entry is 'bumped off' the end each time a record
   with a greater value is inserted.

3. When your pass of the file is complete, your table contains the top
   N records, in decreasing order of sales.

Insertion sort is easy.  Starting at the end of the table, you shift
'down' toward the end of the table each entry that is lower than the
entry you are inserting.  Keep shifting entries down until you find
table(sub) >= the new entry, or sub decrements to zero.  Then insert
the new entry into table(sub + 1).

However, if you want to sort the whole file without the SORT verb,
for general purpose use I prefer Shell sort.  Note that to sort a large
number of records you may not be able to load all of them into a memory.
However, if the disk records are fixed length, you may be able to read
the amounts and record number for the amount into a table and when
complete read the records off disk from the record numbers in the
table, and write the records into a new file in sorted order.  Else,
you will need to sort the file in sections, then merge all the
sections when complete.

      /*****************************************************************
      *                                                                *
      *               S H E L L   S O R T   T A B L E                  *
      *                                                                *
      ******************************************************************
      *
       01  SORT-TABLE.
           78  ST-ELIM                 VALUE 10000.
           03  ST-ELEMENTS             VALUE SPACES.
               05  ST-ELEMENT          OCCURS ST-ELIM TIMES.
                   07  ST-ELEMENT-KEY.
                       09  ST-ENT-FLD1     PIC  9(06).
      ****             09  ST-ENT-FLD?     PIC  ?(??).
      ****         07  ST-ELEMENT-OTHER    PIC  ?(??).
      *
           03  ST-SAVE                 VALUE SPACES.
               07  ST-SAVE-KEY.
                   09  ST-SAVE-FLD1    PIC  9(06).
      ****         09  ST-SAVE-FLD?    PIC  ?(??).
      ****     07  ST-SAVE-OTHER       PIC  ?(??).
      *
           03  ST-EMAX                 PIC  9(05) BINARY VALUE 0.
           03  ST-I                    PIC  9(05) BINARY VALUE 0.
           03  ST-J                    PIC  9(05) BINARY VALUE 0.
           03  ST-K                    PIC  9(05) BINARY VALUE 0.
           03  ST-L                    PIC  9(05) BINARY VALUE 0.
           03  ST-M                    PIC  9(05) BINARY VALUE 0.
           03  ST-N                    PIC  9(05) BINARY VALUE 0.


      /*****************************************************************
      *                                                                *
      *             S H E L L   S O R T   R O U T I N E                *
      *                                                                *
      *                     Judson D. McClendon                        *
      *                     Sun Valley Systems                         *
      *                     4522 Shadow Ridge Pkwy                     *
      *                     Pinson, AL 35126-2192                      *
      *                        205-680-0460                            *
      *                                                                *
      *    SORTS TABLE ST-ELEMENTS BY KEY ST-ELEMENT-KEY.              *
      *    PLACE ANY FIELDS TO BE USED AS KEYS UNDER ST-ELEMENT-KEY.   *
      *    SIGNED NUMERIC FIELDS MUST BE IN A FORMAT IN WHICH THE      *
      *    BINARY CODE IS ACCORDING TO SORT SEQUENCE, OR USE THEM      *
      *    AS THE ONLY KEY.  (E.G. IF KEY FIELD IS S9(6), ADD          *
      *    1000000 TO THE VALUE AND STORE IT AS A PIC 9(7) FIELD.)     *
      *    FOR DESCENDING FIELDS, STORE THEM WITH INVERTED COLLATING   *
      *    ORDER (E.G. FOR ALPHA FIELDS, XOR THEM WITH X"FF").         *
      *    AVOID USAGE WHERE BYTE ORDER IS REVERSED.  DISPLAY IS       *
      *    USUALLY SAFEST FOR KEY FIELDS.                              *
      *                                                                *
      *    USAGE:  <POPULATE ST-ELEMENT(1) THRU ST-ELEMENT(N)>         *
      *            MOVE <N> TO ST-EMAX.                                *
      *            PERFORM 007000-SHELLSORT                            *
      *               THRU 007000-EXIT.                                *
      *                                                                *
      *    RESULT: ST-ELEMENT(1) TO ST-ELEMENT(N) IN ASCENDING ORDER.  *
      *                                                                *
      ******************************************************************
      *
       007000-SHELLSORT.
      *
           MOVE ST-EMAX TO ST-N
                           ST-M.
      *
           PERFORM 007010-LOOPER
              THRU 007010-EXIT.
      *
       007000-EXIT.
           EXIT.
      *
      *
      *                       L O O P E R
      *
       007010-LOOPER.
      *
           IF (ST-M > 1)
               DIVIDE 2 INTO ST-M
               COMPUTE ST-K = ST-N - ST-M
               MOVE 1 TO ST-J
               PERFORM 007020-MAKE-PASS
                  THRU 007020-EXIT
               GO TO 007010-LOOPER.
      *
       007010-EXIT.
           EXIT.
      *
      *
      *                    M A K E   P A S S
      *
       007020-MAKE-PASS.
      *
           IF (ST-J NOT > ST-K)
               COMPUTE ST-L = ST-J + ST-M
               MOVE ST-ELEMENT(ST-L) TO ST-SAVE
               MOVE ST-J TO ST-I
               PERFORM 007030-SCAN
                  THRU 007030-EXIT
               MOVE ST-SAVE TO ST-ELEMENT(ST-L)
               ADD 1 TO ST-J
               GO TO 007020-MAKE-PASS.
      *
       007020-EXIT.
           EXIT.
      *
      *
      *                         S C A N
      *
       007030-SCAN.
      *
           IF (ST-ELEMENT-KEY(ST-I) > ST-SAVE-KEY)
               MOVE ST-ELEMENT(ST-I) TO ST-ELEMENT(ST-L)
               MOVE ST-I TO ST-L
               IF (ST-I > ST-M)
                   SUBTRACT ST-M FROM ST-I
                   GO TO 007030-SCAN.
      *
       007030-EXIT.
           EXIT.
```

#### ↳ Re: Bubble Sort

- **From:** "Karl Wagner" <kewagner@home.com>
- **Date:** 1999-03-25T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<77iK2.2473$v4.9062@news.rdc1.on.wave.home.com>`
- **References:** `<36F9A9A2.AF91C68F@wworx.net>`

```
Why not use an 'ORDER BY' clause in your SQL with a descending key to
pull the top 1000 off (in highest-to-lowest order) then feed that to
your report program. It depends on the data and defined indices as to
whether or not it's more efficient but it's a whole lot easier to
maintain.

get

> report generator.  Any help?

#### ↳ Re: Bubble Sort

- **From:** redsky@ibm.net (Thane Hubbell)
- **Date:** 1999-03-25T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<36f9bb94.52447717@news1.ibm.net>`
- **References:** `<36F9A9A2.AF91C68F@wworx.net>`

```
On Wed, 24 Mar 1999 22:12:34 -0500, John DeWitt <kadet@wworx.net>
wrote:

>This is a multi-part message in MIME format.
>--------------E32F66E2C957BF710135B233
…[7 more quoted lines elided]…
>report generator.  Any help?

I'm a relative SQL newbe - but I've been doing a lot lately.  Why not
declare a cursor, with an "ORDER BY" in the sequence you require, then
Fetch 1000 times and then close the cursor?
```

```
go to http://www.etk.com then papers, then sorting to find
a bubble sort, but don't use bubble sort as it is slow.
Use the much faster combsort (also described there).

Aslo, you can use an ORDER BY clause in your SQL.

…[11 more quoted lines elided]…

<a id="t-df543c8e56"></a>
## Thread: COBOL/MVS Migration — 3 this month / 4 total

_Topics: [`Migration and conversion`](topics.md#migration) · [`Mainframe, z/OS, JCL, CICS`](topics.md#mainframe)_

### COBOL/MVS Migration

- **From:** Haribee <haribawa@my-dejanews.com>
- **Date:** 1999-03-24T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7dbcrc$k0r$1@nnrp1.dejanews.com>`

```
I would appreciate if anyone can provide information on any surprises to
expect when migrating from "COBOL II Rel. 4" to "COBOL for MVS".

According to COBOL for MVS & VM V1 Rel. 2 Compiler and Run-Time Migration
Guide, COBOL/MVS is "upward compatible" from COBOL II Rel. 4, so long as the
COBOL II code was compiled with NOCMPR2.

Also, We are using run-time LE/370 libraries, CICS V4.1, and DB2 V5.

-----------== Posted via Deja News, The Discussion Network ==----------
http://www.dejanews.com/       Search, Read, Discuss, or Start Your Own
```

#### ↳ Re: COBOL/MVS Migration

- **From:** scomstock@aol.com (S Comstock)
- **Date:** 1999-03-24T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<19990324145552.16733.00001072@ng124.aol.com>`
- **References:** `<7dbcrc$k0r$1@nnrp1.dejanews.com>`

```
Haribee <haribawa@my-dejanews.com> writes ...

>I would appreciate if anyone can provide information on any surprises to
>expect when migrating from "COBOL II Rel. 4" to "COBOL for MVS".
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
>Also, We are using run-time LE/370 libraries, CICS V4.1, and DB2 V5.

Go to our website (www.trainersfriend.com), select Topics, Select COBOL; read
the overview there; then select Beyond COBOL II; this course description gives
you a big picture overview; at the bottom of the page is a link to the course
objectives, and then to the detailed topical outline; the outline should give
you the next level of detail.

After that, you should bring us in to train your people, of course.

Cheers,


Steve Comstock
Telephone: 303-393-8716
www.trainersfriend.com
email: steve@trainersfriend.com
256-B S. Monaco Parkway
Denver, CO 80224
USA
```

#### ↳ Re: COBOL/MVS Migration

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 1999-03-27T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7dia2u$7eb@dfw-ixnews6.ix.netcom.com>`
- **References:** `<7dbcrc$k0r$1@nnrp1.dejanews.com>`

```
1) Read *both* the COBOL migration guide *and* the LE migration guide (from
IBM)

2) There are minimal (a few reserved word) differences between the 2
compilers. (In fact, you can even keep using CMPR2 - despite what you say).

3) There is a WORLD of difference between running with the VS COBOL II
run-time library and the LE run-time library - even for your existing VS
COBOL II created object code.

I strongly suggest that you plan on migrating your run-time library and
getting it quite STABLE before you introduce the new compiler into your
production environment.
```

---

<a id="t-00c73cdd97"></a>
## Thread: New Jersey's Woes - An Apology, Corrected Example — 10 msgs

### New Jersey's Woes - An Apology, Corrected Example

- **From:** docdwarf@clark.net ()
- **Date:** 1999-03-24T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.software.year-2000,comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7dasvf$5la$1@callisto.clark.net>`

```

First and foremost I must apologise to all whose time I wasted with my
useless example yesterday; two things need to be clarified:

1) the example was a copy sent to a friend to demonstrate to him how
remediation could introduce subtle bugs; it was *not* an attempt to
demonstrate precisely how the NJ Woe came about.

2) In my haste and sloppiness I gave an example which would not compile
because of unmatched scope-delimiters... I tried to Make Things Neat by
adhering to COBOLII/COBOL '85 standards rather than Maintain Accuracy by
giving an example of code which would willy-nilly combine the various
aspects of COBOL and COBOLII/COBOL '85 standards.

The only defense I can ofer is a variant of the Brooklyn Bridge defense
('everyone else is doing it'); I have seen examples in textbooks which
would not compile... but their sloppiness is no excuse for mine.

The corrected example is below:

--begin corrected code

MOVE MFCS-CUST-NO-01 TO WS-CUSTNO.
IF INDEPENDENT-CUSTOMER
    PERFORM A53102-ALLOCATE-LOT THRU A53102-EXIT
    PERFORM C88092-CALC-CUBES   THRU C88092-EXIT
    PERFORM E35702-FIND-SHPWGHT THRU E35702-EXIT
    INSPECT WS-CUSTNO TALLYING WS-PERFORM-SUB FOR ALL 'QZ'
    IF WS-PERFORM-SUB > 0 AND WS-DT-REC-ADDED < WS-CUTOFF
        MOVE 'Y'  TO SPECL-CUST-FLAG
        PERFORM B28095-PROCESS-SPECUST THRU B28095-EXIT
         VARYING SUB1 FROM WS-PERFORM-SUB BY 1
          UNTIL SUB1 > WS-PERFORM-SUB
    END-IF
END-IF.

MOVE MFCS-CUST-NO-01 TO WS-CUSTNO.
IF INDEPENDENT-CUSTOMER
    PERFORM A53102-ALLOCATE-LOT THRU A53102-EXIT
    PERFORM C88092-CALC-CUBES   THRU C88092-EXIT
    PERFORM E35702-FIND-SHPWGHT THRU E35702-EXIT
    INSPECT WS-CUSTNO TALLYING WS-PERFORM-SUB FOR ALL 'QZ'
    IF WS-DT-REC-ADDED-YY < 40
        MOVE 20 TO WS-DT-REC-ADDED-CC
    ELSE
        MOVE 19 TO WS-DT-REC-ADDED-CC
    END-IF
    IF WS-CUTOFF-YY < 40
        MOVE 20 TO WS-CUTOFF-CC
    ELSE
        MOVE 19 TO WS-CUTOFF-CC
    END-IF.
    IF WS-PERFORM-SUB > 0 AND WS-DT-REC-ADDED-CCYYMMDD < WS-CUTOFF-CCYYMMDD
        MOVE 'Y'  TO SPECL-CUST-FLAG
        PERFORM B28095-PROCESS-SPECUST THRU B28095-EXIT
         VARYING SUB1 FROM WS-PERFORM-SUB BY 1
          UNTIL SUB1 > WS-PERFORM-SUB.

--end corrected code.

(also note that if one so desires the two END-IFs which terminate the
unremediated code can also be removed if a period is placed at the end of
the PERFORM... I will leave it to the readers, kind as they have been in
showing me my error, to debate the 'fairness' of doing this or not as it
could be construed as a real Dialect Change between the samples)

Once again, my apologies to all; if nothing else I have once again
demonstrated that these free examples are worth exactly what one pays for
them.

DD
```

## Thread: Cobol Brain Teaser — 13 msgs

### Cobol Brain Teaser

- **From:** redsky@ibm.net (Thane Hubbell)
- **Date:** 1999-03-24T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<36f8629c.86866840@news1.ibm.net>`

```
Here is a good one for you.  

This is a program example from my book, Sams Teach Yourself COBOL in
24 hours - demonstrating different data types.  I had a reader write
saying that it would not compile - yet they were trying the EXACT
source I included with the CD.  I took this listing, copied in the
source, and it DID compile fine.  They have installed correctly and
there is not a bug in the compiler.  Why is this error message issued?

First, I know the answer.  Everything you need to solve this is in
this listing.  Have at it.  It took me 45 minutes to solve.  Someone
beat that time.




COBOL85 V30L10
TUE MAR 23 17:35:15 1999  0001


** OPTIONS SPECIFIED **


MESSAGE
SOURCE

000460 @OPTIONS MAIN


** OPTIONS ESTABLISHED **


  ALPHAL                NOEQUALS                  MESSAGE
RSV(ALL)                TAB(8)                
  BINARY(WORD,MLBON)      FLAG(I)                 NCW(STD)
SDS                   NOTEST                  
NOCHECK                 NOFLAGSW                  NSPCOMP(NSP)
SOURCE(1)             NOTRACE                 
NOCONF                    LANGLVL(85)           NONUMBER
SRF(VAR,VAR)          NOTRUNC                 
NOCOPY                    LINECOUNT(60)           OBJECT
SSIN(SYSIN)             ZWB                   
  CURRENCY(S)             LINESIZE(136)         NOOPTIMIZE
SSOUT(SYSOUT)                                 
NODLOAD                   MAIN                    QUOTE
STD1(JIS2)                                    



COBOL85 V30L10  CHAPT03B
TUE MAR 23 17:35:15 1999  0002


       LINE   SEQNO  A   B


          2   000470 Identification Division.
          3   000480 Program-Id.  Chapt03b.
          4   000490 Environment Division.
          5   000500 Configuration Section.
          6   000510 Source-Computer.  IBM-PC.
          7   000520 Object-Computer.  IBM-PC.
          8   000530 Data Division.
          9   000540 Working-Storage Section.
         10   000550 01  Group-Level-Item.
         11   000560     05  Elementary-Numeric       Pic 9(7)
Value 12345.
         12   000570     05  Elementary-Numeric-Dec   Pic 9(5)v99
Value 123.45.
         13   000580     05  Elementary-Numeric-Sign  Pic S9(5)v99
Value -123.45.
         14   000590 01  Edited-Group-Item.
         15   000600     05  Elementary-Zero-Sup      Pic Z(6)9.
         16   000610     05  Elementary-Aster-Sup     Pic ******9.
         17   000620     05  Elementary-Edited        Pic Z,Z(3),Z(3).
         18   000630     05  Elementary-Edited-Dec    Pic
Z,Z(3),Z(3).99.
         19   000640 01  Group-Alphanumeric-Item.
         20   000650     05  Elementary-Alphanum      Pic X(20)
         21   000660         Value "ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRST".
         22   000670     05  Elementary-Alphanum-A    Pic X(6)
         23   000680         Value "UVWXYZ".
         24   000690 01  Group-Alphanumeric-Edited.
         25   000700     05  Edited-Alphanumeric      Pic
X(3)/X(3)/X(3).
         26   000710 Procedure Division.
         27   000720 Chapt03b-Start.
         28   000730     Move Elementary-Numeric     To
Elementary-Zero-Sup.
         29   000740     Move Elementary-Numeric     To
Elementary-Edited.
         30   000750     Move Elementary-Numeric     To
Elementary-Aster-Sup.
         31   000760     Move Elementary-Numeric-Dec To
Elementary-Edited-Dec.
         32   000770     Move Elementary-Alphanum    To
Edited-Alphanumeric.
         33   000780     Display "1 Group Alphanumeric="
Group-Alphanumeric-Item.
         34   000790     Display "2 Elementary Alpha="
Elementary-Alphanum.
         35   000800     Display "3 Elementary Alpha A="
Elementary-Alphanum-A.
         36   000810     Display "4 Edited Alphanumeric="
Edited-Alphanumeric.
         37   000820     Display "5 Group Level Item="
Group-Level-Item.
         38   000830     Display "6 Elementary Numeric="
Elementary-Numeric.
         39   000840     Display "7 Elementary Numeric Dec="
Elementary-Numeric-Dec.
         40   000850     Display "8 Elementary Numeric Sign="
Elementary-Numeric-Sign.
         41   000860     Display "9 Elementary Zero Sup="
Elementary-Zero-Sup.
         42   000870     Display "10 Elementary Aster Sup="
Elementary-Aster-Sup.
         43   000880     Display "11 Elementary Edited="
Elementary-Edited.
         44   000890     Display "12 Elementary Edited Dec="
Elementary-Edited-Dec.
         45   000910     Stop Run.



COBOL85 V30L10  CHAPT03B
TUE MAR 23 17:35:15 1999  0003


** DIAGNOSTIC MESSAGE ** (CHAPT03B)


JMN2040I-S  13  NONNUMERIC LITERAL,FIGURATIVE CONSTANT,OR SYMBOLIC
CHARACTER MUST BE SPECIFIED IN VALUE CLAUSE OF 
            ALPHABETIC ITEM,ALPHANUMERIC ITEM,ALPHANUMERIC EDITED
ITEM,OR NUMERIC EDITED ITEM. FIGURATIVE CONSTANT 
            'SPACE' ASSUMED SPECIFIED.




COBOL85 V30L10  CHAPT03B
TUE MAR 23 17:35:15 1999  0004


** STATISTICS **

  FILE NAME     = C:\FSC\Tycobol\chapt03b.cob
  DATE AND TIME = TUE MAR 23 17:35:15 1999

    SOURCE STATEMENTS              =         45 RECORDS
    PROGRAM SIZE(CODE)             =          0 BYTES
    CONTROL LEVEL                  =          1 LEVEL
    CPU TIME                       =       0.11 SECONDS

  HIGHEST SEVERITY CODE = S

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Cobol Brain Teaser

- **From:** redsky@ibm.net (Thane Hubbell)
- **Date:** 1999-03-24T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<Jl0PnHJ5PvPd-pn2-ikDCYoF3m5ff@Dwight_Miller.iix.com>`
- **References:** `<36f8629c.86866840@news1.ibm.net> <7dapfl$5ko$1@client2.news.psi.net>`

```
On Wed, 24 Mar 1999 13:33:26, "Tom Morrison" <t.morrison@liant.com> 
wrote:

> After looking at it two minutes, I would guess that it has something to do
> with CURRENCY(S) [rather that CURRENCY($)], but I am no expert on Fujitsu
> COBOL listings.
> 
> If indeed S is the currency symbol, then PIC S9(5)v99 is numeric edited.

Yep!  You and Volker got it faster than I did.  I even printed and 
compared my compiler options to this one.  I missed the difference 
between $ and S.  Grrr...

Anyway, Swiss regional settings in Windows 95/98/NT  - have an S for 
currency, which Fujitsu picks up for the default compiler options.

Interestingly, you can override S with $ by specifying CURRENCY($) as 
a compiler option, or you can change your regional settings for 
currency to something other than a COBOL reserved picture.

You CANNOT, however (at least with V3) override a default CURRENCY($) 
with CURRENCY(S).

------

Bug?  I don't think so.  But how does one handle currency in 
Switzerland?  Convert to the Euro?
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Cobol Brain Teaser

- **From:** Daniel Jacot <daniel.jacot@winterthur.ch>
- **Date:** 1999-03-25T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7dcqrh$rdm$1@nnrp1.dejanews.com>`
- **References:** `<36f8629c.86866840@news1.ibm.net> <7dapfl$5ko$1@client2.news.psi.net> <Jl0PnHJ5PvPd-pn2-ikDCYoF3m5ff@Dwight_Miller.iix.com>`

```
In article <Jl0PnHJ5PvPd-pn2-ikDCYoF3m5ff@Dwight_Miller.iix.com>,
  redsky@ibm.net (Thane Hubbell) wrote:
<snip>
> Bug?  I don't think so.  But how does one handle currency in
> Switzerland?  Convert to the Euro?
>

Since only Euro, US-Dollar and UK-Pound have a currency symbol and since we
trade with the whole world ;-) we are used to handle the currencies as the
are called. Our printed reports show 'SFr.' or 'USD' or 'EUR' or 'FFr' or....
The line with amounts in US-Dollar is often marked with the '$' sign, but not
as part of the PIC clause, but rather as a literal. We don't care about the
currency symbols in our programs, normally we leave the '$' sign as it is and
never use it. So it's a bug anyhow if the operating system sets or even
accepts an 'S' as currency symbol. Nobody ever uses this character!

Regards
Daniel
------------------------------------------------------------------------
visit us at http://www.winterthur.com

-----------== Posted via Deja News, The Discussion Network ==----------
http://www.dejanews.com/       Search, Read, Discuss, or Start Your Own
```

comp.lang.cobol thusly:

>The only thing I can think of is that some compilers do not support a picture
>clause of X(3)/X(3)/X(3)

There were Cobol-68 compilers which didn't support that, but any compiler
whihc is compliant with X3.23-1974 or X3.23-1985 will accept PIC
X(3)/X(3)/X(3)
```

On Wed, 24 Mar 1999 03:59:57 GMT, redsky@ibm.net (Thane Hubbell) wrote:

Okay, lets see:

t=00:00

Pasted the source into an spf window, readjust the columns, check if everything is in
correct margins.  It is.  Compile.  It works, using visiual age for cobol

t=00:10

Scratch head.  Remove splinters from fingertips. Scratch again.  Program as written is
correct.  Conclusion: Problem must be somewhere else.

t=00:15  According to the gospel of thane, the listing contains all the required info.
What info is there:  Compiler Options, Source, and Error messages.  Source is okay, Error
messages come in too late.  Must be compiler options...

t=00:20  Stop the clock.  I will have to install the Fujitsu compiler

t=00:22 Start the clock again, no need to install.  Remember, everything is to be found in
the listing.  We don't need no stinking docs....


>  ALPHAL                NOEQUALS                  MESSAGE
>RSV(ALL)                TAB(8)                
…[7 more quoted lines elided]…
>SSIN(SYSIN)             ZWB

none of these rings a bell
                   
>  CURRENCY(S)  

Is this a typo or deliberate or dos my newsread act up.  If this is S, then S9(5)V99 is
numeric edited, and will not accept the value

BUT - on my Visual Age compiler, a currency sign of S is rejected as being invalid.
Actually the docs say the the currency sign cannot be any of

 o Digits zero (0) through nine (9) 

 o Uppercase alphabetic characters A B C D E G N P R S V X Z, or their lowercase
equivalents 

 o The space 

 o Special characters * + - / , . ; ( ) " = ' 

 o A figurative constant 

 o A null-terminated literal 

 o A DBCS literal 
 
Well, either the IBM compiler is more restrictive than Fujitsu, or the Fujitsu compiler
does have a bug, or currency(S) is not hte problem at all

t=00:45  giving up


with kind regards

Volker Bandke
(BSP GmbH)

## Thread: New user on Fujitsu COBOL V3 help me — 4 this month / 7 total

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

### New user on Fujitsu COBOL V3 help me

- **From:** Alain Chappuis <Alain.Chappuis@medecine.unige.ch>
- **Date:** 1999-03-24T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<36F8A59F.4AEFF02@medecine.unige.ch>`

```
Hello
I'm a new user on Fujitsu COBOL V3 and I need some help.

After I installed the compilator under NT4 SP4, and I reading
the manual "Getting Started with Fujitsu COBOL". In the
application "Programming Staff" I open my file .COB no problem.
But when I trying to use Tools and any option in, in particular
WINCOB[Compile...] it does not occur anything at all! 

No box open for asking me for the file to compile.
Did I forget parameter something? 

Thank you in advance for your help.

Ps: my compiler is installed on c:\Program File\Cobol3 not in
the default proposed by Fujitsu!

Alain
```

#### ↳ Re: New user on Fujitsu COBOL V3 help me

- **From:** Bob Berman <bberman@netbox.com>
- **Date:** 1999-03-24T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<36F9A09B.2A08@netbox.com>`
- **References:** `<36F8A59F.4AEFF02@medecine.unige.ch>`

```
Alain Chappuis wrote:
> But when I trying to use Tools and any option in, in particular
> WINCOB[Compile...] it does not occur anything at all!
> 
> Ps: my compiler is installed on c:\Program File\Cobol3 not in
> the default proposed by Fujitsu!

I made the same mistake the first time I installed it.  V3 will not
handle directory names longer than 8 characters.  If you search the
registry carefully, and change the paths to c:\Progra~1\...you can get
it to work, but I found that it was much easier to reinstall using their
defaults.

Bob
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: New user on Fujitsu COBOL V3 help me

- **From:** "Hajo Schepker" <schepker@geocities.com>
- **Date:** 1999-03-26T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7dgea9$aqn$1@namib.north.de>`
- **References:** `<36F8A59F.4AEFF02@medecine.unige.ch> <36F9A09B.2A08@netbox.com>`

```
V3 can handle directories with more than 8 characters, only watch, if threre
is no space in the directory-name.
```

#### ↳ Re: New user on Fujitsu COBOL V3 help me

- **References:** `<36F8A59F.4AEFF02@medecine.unige.ch>`

```
You have to set up a project ... the pstaff program is a full project
manager with projects, editor, etc. all built in.  Try opening a project
(use one of the samples), then compiling.  Once you see that, you should
have no problem setting up a project, and sticking your own Cobol into it.

## Thread: RM/COBOL vs. 6.5 prob. - retn code — 4 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

### RM/COBOL vs. 6.5 prob. - retn code

- **From:** Erie <73042.311@CompuServe.COM>
- **Date:** 1999-03-24T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<uQyQZzid#GA.96@nih2naaf.prod2.compuserve.com>`

```
I am writing a windows 95 (DOS) batch file which will call several 
COBOL programs.  I know that RM/COBOL gives you a return code in 
the program during an error.  The question I have is how can I use 
this return code code to determine if the program has an error?  
It appears that this return code must not be automatically 
returned to the operating system because the batch ERRORLEVEL test 
does not catch the return code.  Doe anyone have any experience 
doing anything like this?  Let me know.  Any help would be greatly 
appreciated.  Thanks in advance.
```

#### ↳ Re: RM/COBOL vs. 6.5 prob. - retn code

- **From:** "Donald Tees" <donald@willmack.com>
- **Date:** 1999-03-24T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7dbpmp$jgb$3@news.igs.net>`
- **References:** `<uQyQZzid#GA.96@nih2naaf.prod2.compuserve.com>`

```
You may be running into the the old problem of the dos IF statement.  It is
not an equality check, it is a less than check. You have to start with the
highest level first.
IE
if errorlevel 1 goto
if errorlevel 2 goto
will not work.
but
if errorlevel 2 goto
if errorlevel 1 goto
will
Erie <73042.311@CompuServe.COM> wrote in message ...
>I am writing a windows 95 (DOS) batch file which will call several
>COBOL programs.  I know that RM/COBOL gives you a return code in
…[6 more quoted lines elided]…
>appreciated.  Thanks in advance.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: RM/COBOL vs. 6.5 prob. - retn code

- **From:** Erie <73042.311@CompuServe.COM>
- **Date:** 1999-03-25T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<eUJ89bsd#GA.255@nih2naad.prod2.compuserve.com>`
- **References:** `<7dbpmp$jgb$3@news.igs.net>`

```
Thanks, but I don't think this is the problem because I was just 
using NOT ERRORLEVEL to test if the value was not 0.  So, I don't 
believe RMCOBOL is really returning any value back to the 
operating system.  But, thanks for trying to help.
```

#### ↳ Re: RM/COBOL vs. 6.5 prob. - retn code

- **From:** "Robert Heady" <r.heady@liant.com>
- **Date:** 1999-03-25T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<vXsK2.64216$rs2.18430163@client.news.psi.net>`
- **References:** `<uQyQZzid#GA.96@nih2naaf.prod2.compuserve.com>`

```
Erie,

This works for me.


ID DIVISION.
PROGRAM-ID.  A.
REMARKS.   Returns a return-code of 2.
PROCEDURE DIVISION.
A.
      MOVE 2 TO RETURN-CODE.
      STOP RUN.
END PROGRAM A.


START /W RUNCOBOL  A.COB  K
IF ERRORLEVEL 252 GOTO LEVEL252
IF ERRORLEVEL 2   GOTO LEVEL2
IF ERRORLEVEL 1   GOTO LEVEL1
ECHO  Error level 1  2 or 252 not returned
PAUSE
GOTO ENDIT

:LEVEL252
CLS
ECHO   Program not Found ERROR LEVEL 252
PAUSE
GOTO ENDIT

:LEVEL2
CLS
ECHO   Test PASSED  ERROR LEVEL 2
PAUSE
GOTO ENDIT

:LEVEL1
CLS
ECHO   Test FAILED ERROR LEVEL 1
PAUSE
:ENDIT

==============================================
Robert A. Heady                                   r.heady@liant.com

Liant Software Corp.
8911 Capital of Texas Hwy.
Austin, TX  78759
Phone:   (512) 343-1010
Fax:       (800) 835-0301
Home Page:  www.liant.com                                     VRWC
==============================================
Erie <73042.311@CompuServe.COM> wrote in message ...
>I am writing a windows 95 (DOS) batch file which will call several
>COBOL programs.  I know that RM/COBOL gives you a return code in
…[6 more quoted lines elided]…
>appreciated.  Thanks in advance.
```

matt wrote:
> 
> Or if you have a really old version of COBOL you could use the TRANSFORM verb
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
>         TRANSFORM WS-DATA-NAME FROM ZERO TO SPACE.

This was available for both ANSI 68 & ANSI 72, so in a shop which had
both, I didn't change EXAMINE to INSPECT, but instead changed EXAMINE to
TRANSFORM where I could.  I thought I was being smart, allowing the
programs to use the more stable verb!!!
```

_Topics: [`Language features and syntax`](topics.md#syntax) · [`Tutorials, books, learning`](topics.md#learning)_

### Student question "evaluate"

- **From:** "Bill" <123vapos123@excite.com>
- **Date:** 1999-03-24T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7db8ej$qqr@chronicle.concentric.net>`

I'm using the "EVALUATE TRUE" statement
When a true condition is hit upon, does the program execute this condition
and jump to the "END-EVALUATE" or does it fall to the next WHEN?

case 1 :  A=2, B=5, C=0;  only 456-WALK will be executed.
case 2 : A=1, B=8, C=9;  will 123-RUN and 789-CRAWL both execute?

EVALUATE TRUE
    WHEN A = 1
      PERFORM 123-RUN THROUGH 123-EXIT
    WHEN B = 5
      PERFORM 456-WALK THROUGH 456-EXIT
    WHEN C = 9
      PERFORM 789-CRAWL THROUGH 789-EXIT
END-EVALUATE

Thanks in advance.
Bill
________________________
remove both "123"s to email me
123vapos123@excite.com
```

```
bob7536@aol.com (Bob7536) wrote:
>Is anyone aware of a Win32 API that will tell you what programs are running on
>the computer ?

This isn't really a COBOL question, but on NT 4.0 there is 
EnumProcesses().

Steve.
```

#### ↳ Re: Win32 API with NetExpress

- **From:** "Karl Wagner" <kewagner@home.com>
- **Date:** 1999-03-24T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<l67K2.501$v4.505@news.rdc1.on.wave.home.com>`
- **References:** `<19990324084348.13537.00003457@ng127.aol.com>`

```
Take a look at the EnumWindows function. It enumerates all top-level
windows returning a window handle. You have to write a callback function
to do something with the window handle, like convert it to a file name,
but there's an article in the knowledge base on how to do that. The
sample program pview.c has all the code needed. Good luck wading through
it. I gave up after the first few hundred lines.



Bob7536 <bob7536@aol.com> wrote in message
news:19990324084348.13537.00003457@ng127.aol.com...

running on

>
> Thanks,
> Bob Hennessey
>
```

## Thread: idcams — 9 msgs

### idcams

- **From:** Rico <RA-Diaz@wiu.edu>
- **Date:** 1999-03-25T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<36F9D51D.A1AF363F@wiu.edu>`

```
i'm new to cobol (second semester student) and was given a 15 minute
course on file allocation with idcams on an MVS mainframe....

could anyone give me some ideas on some good resources for more info on
idcams, cuz i have no clue what i'm doin and don't wanna bomb the
program

thanks
```

#### ↳ Re: idcams

- **From:** Simon Martens <smart@sunshine.nl>
- **Date:** 1999-03-27T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<36FC2A7D.D15037EE@sunshine.nl>`
- **References:** `<36F9D51D.A1AF363F@wiu.edu>`

```
Idcams is a JCL utility (JCL = Job Control Language, the underlying
embedding of your Cobol programs on MVS). It is the utility you need
to create and handle VSAM files.

There are hardly any books on JCL. The best ones are:
Gary DeWard Brown - System 390 JCL; 4th edition; Wiley (www.wiley.
  com/compbooks/) and
Doug Lowe - MVS JCL; 2d edition; Murach.
Of course you can find details about both books at www.amazon.com.

- Simon -

Rico wrote:
> 
> i'm new to cobol (second semester student) and was given a 15 minute
…[6 more quoted lines elided]…
> thanks
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: idcams

- **From:** Dave Jakubowsky <jakubowsky.dave@mcleod.net>
- **Date:** 1999-03-26T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<36FC4AD7.324530FE@mcleod.net>`
- **References:** `<36F9D51D.A1AF363F@wiu.edu> <36FC2A7D.D15037EE@sunshine.nl>`

```
Lowe also has a small book
VSAM for the Cobol programmer, 2nd edition
only a couple hundred pages, but fairly useful.

Simon Martens wrote:

> Idcams is a JCL utility (JCL = Job Control Language, the underlying
> embedding of your Cobol programs on MVS). It is the utility you need
…[19 more quoted lines elided]…
> > thanks
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: idcams

- **From:** William Lynch <wblynch@att.net>
- **Date:** 1999-03-27T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<36FC7B1C.1ADBA717@att.net>`
- **References:** `<36F9D51D.A1AF363F@wiu.edu> <36FC2A7D.D15037EE@sunshine.nl>`

```
Simon Martens wrote:
> 
> Idcams is a JCL utility 

Simon is wrong here. IDCAMS is part of DFP (Data Facility Product, I
think) and is generally known as "Access method Services". JCL is
another 'nother smoke. One way to invoke IDCAMS is via JCL, but that's
not the only way.

Bill Lynch

PS: I'm no expert on all the bits of what we refer to as "MVS". If
anyone's interested in a proper definition and explanation of the
relationship of the various products, I'd suggest trying IBM's site for
online doc or the IBM-MAIN NG.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: idcams

- **From:** "Joel C. Ewing" <jcewing@acm.org>
- **Date:** 1999-03-31T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3701BBC0.69233C4@acm.org>`
- **References:** `<36F9D51D.A1AF363F@wiu.edu> <7do43a$87t$1@nnrp1.dejanews.com>`

```


wtsquid@my-dejanews.com wrote:
> 
> In article <36F9D51D.A1AF363F@wiu.edu>,
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
> > program

> The best source of information I have found is the IBM publication 'Access
> Method Services for VSAM Catalogs' pub no SC26-4905-01, costs approximately
> $30- $40. Each business I work for, if they don't already have it, I get them
> to purchase it, no matter if I am working as a DBA or programmer.
> 

The manual you probably want is "Access Method Services for ICF
Catalogs", not the one for VSAM catalogs.  VSAM Catalogs are obsolete
(they are non-Y2K compliant and are designed to refuse to work after
1999-12-31).  VSAM Catalogs have been functionally stabilized for years
with all enhancements only going to ICF (Integrated Catalog Facility)
catalog structures.  If you are at a shop that still uses any VSAM
Catalogs, they have barely 9 months to convert.
```

#### ↳ Re: idcams

- **From:** "ChrisOsborne" <chris_n_osborne@yahoo.com>
- **Date:** 1999-03-29T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<1WML2.1244$UC.6392@news.uswest.net>`
- **References:** `<36F9D51D.A1AF363F@wiu.edu>`

```
The best resource I know of for the beginner is VSAM: Access Method
Services and Application Programming, by Doug Lowe (the same author
that wrote the JCL book mentioned in another post on this thread),
published by Mike Murach & Associates (www.murach.com)
This book covers the majority of what you will use in VSAM.  IBM's
manual, also already mentioned, will get you the rest of the
information you need.

There is also another book, also by Murach, and recently revised, VSAM
for the COBOL Programmer.  This book is shorter than the first, and
leaves out the section on creating MVS catalogs and using VSAM with
MVS Assembler, and has been re-written in a more modern style, and
appears to concentrate solely on interaction with COBOL programs; but
I don't own it and haven't read it, so I can't vouch for it.

(When I say re-written, I mean in the last ten years, some of the
Murach books date back to the middle eighties, although they're still
good; they've just released new editions of their first DB2 book, and
another is coming in a couple of months.  Hopefully, this will signal
a complete mainframe book line revision.)



Rico <RA-Diaz@wiu.edu> wrote in message
news:36F9D51D.A1AF363F@wiu.edu...
> i'm new to cobol (second semester student) and was given a 15 minute
> course on file allocation with idcams on an MVS mainframe....
>
> could anyone give me some ideas on some good resources for more info
on
> idcams, cuz i have no clue what i'm doin and don't wanna bomb the
> program
>
> thanks
>
```

Small correction
ODBC is NOT only for database connections.

As for the connection it would be exactly the same as for the other
connections (with small corrections depending on the ODBC driver).

platform.

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Coul someone send me the ODBC cobol driver for Visual Basic.

_(thread depth: 4)_

- **From:** "Tom Morrison" <t.morrison@liant.com>
- **Date:** 1999-03-29T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7doirc$ka3$1@client2.news.psi.net>`
- **References:** `<36FA0FF2.1600104F@sge.it> <GGUK2.1442$7k3.3974@news.uswest.net> <36fcfad7.513974@news.indigo.ie> <aYML2.1245$UC.5745@news.uswest.net>`

```
ChrisOsborne wrote in message ...
>What else is ODBC used for?

I think the confusion here is that some people don't consider the collection
of files managed by a COBOL application as a database, though it clearly is.

What the collection seems not to be is a "relational" database.  ODBC can be
used to connect to COBOL data files if the data therein can somehow be made
to look like a relational database.

Relativity, a product of Liant Software Corporation, can do this job quite
nicely; Frederico is aware of this, and is trying to elicit the information
necessary to help the original poster.

Information about Relativity can be found at our web site.

Tom Morrison
www.liant.com

>
>
…[30 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

#### ↳ Re: Coul someone send me the ODBC cobol driver for Visual Basic.

- **From:** areymond@csi.com (Alain Reymond)
- **Date:** 1999-03-30T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3700800f.5002022@news.skynet.be>`
- **References:** `<36FA0FF2.1600104F@sge.it>`

```
An ODBC for which target? This is the most important thing to know.
Seen from VB, the ODBC API or interface is unique. But is you have the
appropriate driver, you can access an Access, Oracle, SQLServer
database or even text files, Cobol Isam files.
There are dozens of ODBC drivers available. For a relative complete
view of what is avalaible, go to www.merant.com. They are the owner of
DataDirect which is probably the leader for the edition of ODBC
drivers.

Yours faithfully,

Alain Reymond


On Thu, 25 Mar 1999 11:29:06 +0100, Luca Ruggiero <lruggiero@sge.it>
wrote:

>Tank.
>
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Coul someone send me the ODBC cobol driver for Visual Basic.

- **From:** areymond@csi.com (Alain Reymond)
- **Date:** 1999-03-31T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3701cc74.519646@news.skynet.be>`
- **References:** `<36FA0FF2.1600104F@sge.it> <3700800f.5002022@news.skynet.be> <37012A9F.1FF71572@siber.com>`

```
Vadim,

With MF, the ODBC was an adventure. They had first Correlate, a
product that they dropt out after a few months of commercialization.
They externalized their ODBC driver to Transoft, a UK based company.
Now, they bought Intersolv, the leader in ODBC driver. So, I hope that
they will develop their own solution for accessing their VSAM files -
if there is no contra-indication due to some commercial agreement with
other companies.
You can find ODBC drivers for MF at Transoft, Parkaway Software,
Liant. According to me, all these solutions seems to be expensive
compared to the price of a single ODBC driver at Intersolv.
I am waiting for MF to announce an ODBC driver in the near future. It
would be logical, isn't it?

Alain Reymond

wrote:

…[8 more quoted lines elided]…

<a id="t-ad2f74d9b2"></a>
## Thread: Getting System date in 4 digit year format — 14 msgs

_Topics: [`Y2K and Year-2000 remediation`](topics.md#y2k) · [`Date and calendar processing`](topics.md#dates)_

### Getting System date in 4 digit year format

- **From:** "Duane Dobesh" <dobesh@mindspring.com>
- **Date:** 1999-03-25T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7dd81g$dmc$1@camel18.mindspring.com>`

```
Can someone tell me what is the easiest way to get the system date in 4
digit year format.  I use MF COBOL.    ACCEPT ... from date seems to only
provide the year in YY format.

I apologize if this question has been asked and answers probably multiple
times before.

Thanks
```

#### ↳ Re: Getting System date in 4 digit year format

- **From:** Warren Porter <warrenp@ASPMnetdoor.com>
- **Date:** 1999-03-25T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<36FA46AB.4212A77E@ASPMnetdoor.com>`
- **References:** `<7dd81g$dmc$1@camel18.mindspring.com>`

```
In RM-COBOL you can write ACCEPT ... FROM CENTURY-DATE.

Duane Dobesh wrote:

> Can someone tell me what is the easiest way to get the system date in 4
> digit year format.  I use MF COBOL.    ACCEPT ... from date seems to only
> provide the year in YY format.
```

#### ↳ Re: Getting System date in 4 digit year format

- **From:** Howard Brazee <brazee@NOSPAMhome.com>
- **Date:** 1999-03-25T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<36FA4636.624C8036@NOSPAMhome.com>`
- **References:** `<7dd81g$dmc$1@camel18.mindspring.com>`

```
Duane Dobesh wrote:
> 
> Can someone tell me what is the easiest way to get the system date in 4
…[6 more quoted lines elided]…
> Thanks


If you're only concerned with today's date, you should be able to use a
window effectively:
    if yy=99 then yyyy=9999 else yyyy=yy + 2000

Or wait until January and add 2000 to it!!!

Or get a compiler which handles YYYY format.  Does MF Cobol have date
functions?
```

#### ↳ Re: Getting System date in 4 digit year format

- **From:** "Leif Svalgaard" <lsvalg@ibm.net>
- **Date:** 1999-03-25T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<36fa2690@news3.us.ibm.net>`
- **References:** `<7dd81g$dmc$1@camel18.mindspring.com>`

```

Duane Dobesh <dobesh@mindspring.com> wrote in message
news:7dd81g$dmc$1@camel18.mindspring.com...
> Can someone tell me what is the easiest way to get the system date in 4
> digit year format.  I use MF COBOL.    ACCEPT ... from date seems to only
> provide the year in YY format.

01  system-date  pic 9(8).
01  filler               redefines system-date.
      02  system-date-cc  pic 99.
      02  system-date-yymmdd pic 9(6).
      02  filler         redefines system-date-yymmdd.
            03  system-date-yy pic 99.
            03  system-date-mm pic 99.
            03  system-date-dd pic 99.

accept system-date-yymmdd from date
if system-date-yy = 99
     move 19 to system-date-cc
else
    move 20 to system-date-cc
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Getting System date in 4 digit year format

- **From:** Alex Romaniuk <ales.romaniuk@zag.si>
- **Date:** 1999-03-25T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<36FA70A7.4182@zag.si>`
- **References:** `<7dd81g$dmc$1@camel18.mindspring.com> <36fa2690@news3.us.ibm.net>`

```
Leif Svalgaard wrote:
> 
> Duane Dobesh <dobesh@mindspring.com> wrote in message
…[19 more quoted lines elided]…
> .

01 system-date  pic 9(8).

move 19000000 to system-date
accept system-date(3:6) from date
if system-date(3:2) < 50  
   move 20 to system-date(1:2)
end-if
```

#### ↳ Re: Getting System date in 4 digit year format

- **From:** goble@kin.on.net (David. E. Goble)
- **Date:** 1999-03-28T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<36fdbb56.0@duster.adelaide.on.net>`
- **References:** `<7dd81g$dmc$1@camel18.mindspring.com>`

```
"Duane Dobesh" <dobesh@mindspring.com> wrote:
>
>Can someone tell me what is the easiest way to get the system date in 4
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
>
Hi Duane;

Using Stern & Stern

Although the technique for setting up the system date shown on pages
217-218 is correct it is more efficient to make use of the string
function which appears in chapter 16 pages 662-666. Because of the
problems caused by year 2000 problems, COBOL programs should no longer
use the older:

ACCEPT ws-date  FROM DATE.

statement as this only produces a two digitr date (pages 217-218). In
stead the COBOL-85 function:

MOVE FUNCTION CURRENT-DATE TO ws-date

should be used and a tipical paragraph of code might be:

xxxx-Set-System-Date

	MOVE FUNCTION CURRENT-DATE TO ws-date.
	STRING ws-date-day "/"
                            ws-date-month "/"
                            ws-date-year "/"
             	      DELIMITED BY SIZE INTO ws-report-date
             END-STRING.

Anyway I hope this helps.
	




--Regards :                David. E. Goble
                           goble@kin.on.net
           http://www-mugc.cc.monash.edu.au/~degob1/goble
      Po Box 648 Kingscote Kangaroo Island South Australia 5223
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Getting System date in 4 digit year format

- **From:** edwardw355@aol.com (EdwardW355)
- **Date:** 1999-03-29T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<19990329003731.22006.00001587@ng-da1.aol.com>`
- **References:** `<36fdbb56.0@duster.adelaide.on.net>`

```
Or in IBM COBOL for OS/390:

01  todays-date.
    03  todays-ccyy             pic  9(04).
    03  todays-mm                pic  9(02).
    03  todays-dd                 pic  9(02).

accept todays-date from date yyyymmdd.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Getting System date in 4 digit year format

- **From:** Nigel Eaton <nigele@rcav8r.demon.co.uk>
- **Date:** 1999-03-29T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<Xv8koDAvn+$2IwB7@rcav8r.demon.co.uk>`

EdwardW355 <edwardw355@aol.com> writes
>Or in IBM COBOL for OS/390:
>
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
>accept todays-date from date yyyymmdd.

Or in Acucobol-GT

ACCEPT TODAYS-DATE FROM CENTURY DATE.

or, compile with -Zy and

ACCEPT TODAYS-DATE FROM DATE.

Best regards

Nigel
```

<a id="t-48faec15bd"></a>
## Thread: SQL SUBSTRING format in Fujitsu COBOL (PARADOX) — 2 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers) · [`Databases and SQL`](topics.md#databases)_

### SQL SUBSTRING format in Fujitsu COBOL (PARADOX)

- **From:** HiGHBoy <hallh@evangel.edu>
- **Date:** 1999-03-25T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7de85o$523$1@nnrp1.dejanews.com>`

```
Hi,

I'm accessing (read: learning how to deal with) a Paradox database on NT using
Fujitsu Cobol with embedded SQL.  All is fine until I tried a SUBSTRING (field
FROM 8 FOR 4), which is a valid SQL expression in Paradox Je(s)t SQL.  I get a
sqlcode of -3100 and a "syntax error (missing operator) in expression
substring...".  I tried various other forms of SUBSTRING syntax with
less-than-happy results. SUBSTRING(field,8,4) produces "undefined function".
SUBSTR is right out.

What is the format for that? The Fujitsu doc give the word SUBSTRING as a
valid expresion but doesnt give the syntax.

Thanks for any input.

Hayward
Springfield MO

-----
I think we've forgotten the Viking foremost rule of thumb: Pillage
before burn.

-----------== Posted via Deja News, The Discussion Network ==----------
http://www.dejanews.com/       Search, Read, Discuss, or Start Your Own
```

#### ↳ Re: SQL SUBSTRING format in Fujitsu COBOL (PARADOX)

- **From:** "G���ran Holm" <goran.holm@enator.se>
- **Date:** 1999-03-30T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7dpuhk$bs1$1@news1.enator.se>`
- **References:** `<7de85o$523$1@nnrp1.dejanews.com>`

```
Try to insert a <space> after comma, ie:
    SUBSTRING(col1, 8, 4)
which is the correct syntax on AS/400

regards
Peter Meister

<a id="t-ebe20150e8"></a>
## Thread: Dynamic assign ? — 10 msgs

### Dynamic assign ?

- **From:** sven_schneider@my-dejanews.com
- **Date:** 1999-03-25T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7ddi99$fm6$1@nnrp1.dejanews.com>`

```
we are working with cobol under mvs.

in the file section is a entry like that:

     select input to sys011

in the jcl-job is declared : //sys011  DD DSN=file.input.data,disp=shr

there are also dd-statements with the names sys012, sys013 and so on. Now we
need a way to change the selection for the input-file at runtime. There is no
way to make a compile if we want to use a other input dataset. Its the same to
the jcl-job. it must be the same job without any changes.
the cobol-program can get the name sys011 or sys012 or ... from a sequential
text-file, which is the only one that could be edited.

Is there a way to make a assign-statement like

     select input to  <name>

and <name> get the value at runtime ?

i knew that this is possible under os/2, and PC/dos but i never heard about
this on MVS.



sven

#### ↳ Re: Dynamic assign ?

- **From:** "Jay Siegel" <ka2csh@nospam.nowhere>
- **Date:** 1999-03-25T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<k2.c2.312f9He4luq40FYt4.NR@nospam.nowhere>`
- **References:** `<7ddi99$fm6$1@nnrp1.dejanews.com>`

```
"ss" == "sven_schneider@my-dejanews.com" writes:

ss> we are working with cobol under mvs.
ss> 
ss> in the file section is a entry like that:
ss> 
ss>      select input to sys011
ss> 
ss> in the jcl-job is declared : //sys011  DD
ss> DSN=file.input.data,disp=shr
ss> 
ss> there are also dd-statements with the names sys012, sys013 and so
ss> on. Now we need a way to change the selection for the input-file
ss> at runtime. There is no way to make a compile if we want to use a
ss> other input dataset. Its the same to the jcl-job. it must be the
ss> same job without any changes. the cobol-program can get the name
ss> sys011 or sys012 or ... from a sequential text-file, which is the
ss> only one that could be edited.
ss> 
ss> Is there a way to make a assign-statement like
ss> 
ss>      select input to  <name>
ss> 
ss> and <name> get the value at runtime ?
ss> 
ss> i knew that this is possible under os/2, and PC/dos but i never
ss> heard about this on MVS.
ss> 
ss> 
ss> 
ss> sven
ss> 
ss> -------------
ss> Sorry, but i'm not a native speaker, thats why my english isn't
ss> the best.
ss> 
ss> 
ss> -----------== Posted via Deja News, The Discussion Network
ss> ==---------- http://www.dejanews.com/       Search, Read,
ss> Discuss, or Start Your Own    

Simple question, simple answer.  Yes, you can do it, and this is how:


1) Write another COBOL program that will be *CALL*ed & name it
DDCHANGE.  In its LINKAGE SECTION, have an 8 character field and a 47
character field, one is NEW-DDNAME and the last field is
FD-TO-BE-CHANGED.  In the PROCEDURE DIVISION, all you do is MOVE
NEW-DDNAME TO the actual DD name in the FD area, which is located in
positions 40 - 47 and then GOBACK.

2) In your currently existing program, at the point you test to want
to dynamically alter the DD name, issue a CALL to *DDCHANGE* USING
NEW-DDNAME and the *FD* name of the DD that you want to change.

Hope that this helps you out.  Please let me know.  You might have to
play around with the length of the FD area to get this one right.  I
did it by writing it to a VB (Variable length record, blocked) file &
using File-Aid to view the record, which contained the FD "record"
area.

Jay
```

- **References:** `<7ddi99$fm6$1@nnrp1.dejanews.com> <k2.c2.312f9He4luq40FYt4.NR@nospam.nowhere>`

#### ↳ Re: Dynamic assign ?

- **From:** "Jay Siegel" <ka2csh@nospam.nowhere>
- **Date:** 1999-03-25T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<t2.m2.312y9o44luq4OGYt4.NR@nospam.nowhere>`
- **References:** `<7ddi99$fm6$1@nnrp1.dejanews.com>`

```
Oh, one other thing about that VB (Variable length file, blocked)
record.  Make sure it's set to 32760 for the maximum possiblity.
You'll need to add 4 to the LRECL and another 4 to the BLKSIZE.  I
gues you don't really need to block since it's only one record
anyways, That way, you'd get another 4 characters, but it (the FD)
definitely won't be this long.


Jay
```

#### ↳ Re: Dynamic assign ?

- **From:** "Jay Siegel" <ka2csh@nospam.nowhere>
- **Date:** 1999-03-25T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<f1.c1.312xBpgSYtq4bmgs4.NR@nospam.nowhere>`
- **References:** `<7ddi99$fm6$1@nnrp1.dejanews.com>`

```
Sven,

    One other item I neglected to mention is that you *MUST* CLOSE
    the FD FILE-NAME prior to CALLing the DDCHANGE program & OPEN it
    again after the call.  You cannot dynamically alter the FD unless
    the file is in a CLOSEd state.


Jay
```

#### ↳ Re: Dynamic assign ?

- **From:** John Trifon <jtrifon@ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 1999-03-25T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<36FB0D2C.5F09C20E@ix.netcom.com>`
- **References:** `<7ddi99$fm6$1@nnrp1.dejanews.com>`

```
Both the examples below allow the COBOL program to execute using a file determined
at execution time. The COBOL program needs only one select and needs no logic for
choosing which file to use. In my opinion, a simple JCL symbolic is preferable to
mucking up a program with code to determine which file to open. Note that the
procs are shown inline for convenience of illustration.

//TESTIT PROC CHOOSE=
//S1            EXEC PGM=YOURPROG
//SYS011      DD DDNAME=&CHOOSE,DISP=SHR
//PICKONE  DD DSN=FIRST.FILE,DISP=SHR
//PICKTWO DD DSN=SECOND.FILE,DISP=SHR
// PEND
//*
//EXEC TESTIT,CHOOSE=PICKONE   /* USE THE FIRST FILE FOR THIS EXECUTION       */
//EXEC  TESTIT,CHOOSE=PICKTWP  /* USE THE SECOND FILE FOR THIS EXECUTION */
....
>>>>OR<<<<<
//TESTIT PROC
//S1       EXEC PGM=YOURPROG
//SYS011 DD DUMMY,DISP=SHR
// PEND
//*
//EXEC TESTIT
//SYS011  DD DSN=FIRST.FILE  /* USE THE FIRST FILE FOR THIS EXECUTION       */
etc...



sven_schneider@my-dejanews.com wrote:

> we are working with cobol under mvs.
>
…[28 more quoted lines elided]…
> http://www.dejanews.com/       Search, Read, Discuss, or Start Your Own
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Dynamic assign ?

- **From:** sven_schneider@my-dejanews.com
- **Date:** 1999-03-26T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7dfnn7$dgv$1@nnrp1.dejanews.com>`
- **References:** `<7ddi99$fm6$1@nnrp1.dejanews.com> <36FB0D2C.5F09C20E@ix.netcom.com>`

```
In article <36FB0D2C.5F09C20E@ix.netcom.com>,
  John Trifon <jtrifon@ix.netcom.com> wrote:
> Both the examples below allow the COBOL program to execute using a file
determined
> at execution time. The COBOL program needs only one select and needs no logic
for
> choosing which file to use. In my opinion, a simple JCL symbolic is
preferable to
> mucking up a program with code to determine which file to open. Note that the
> procs are shown inline for convenience of illustration.
>
 ....
> >>>>OR<<<<<
> //TESTIT PROC
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
> //SYS011  DD DSN=FIRST.FILE  /* USE THE FIRST FILE FOR THIS EXECUTION
*/
> etc...
>

this sample jcl is ok. but the problem is that we cannot change the jcl. the
program that needs the dynamic assign is a subprogram. it could be used by a
main-program or by a another subprogram. the controlling of all programs is
possible with the textfile. this is a relict from older times. the textfile
look like this:

26.03.1999                                       AVK
U                                                USK
file_1 file_2                                 prog1K
1 4 675                                       prog2K
1241 sampletext                               enrg1K


each program can get its own parameters from this file at runtime. the program
with the name 'prog' would get the parameters from the lines that are marked
with prog1K and prog2K at end of line. similar, the program with the name
'enrg' get the parameters from the line marked with 'enrg1K'.

like i wrote, the jcl is unchangeable, but all files that will be possibly
used by the program are defined in the jcl.

at the moment we solved the problem with extra sections for all fileworking
actions. each sections contains a big evaluate that decides which file should
be used. Each file that could be used has its own select .. assign clause in
cobol-program.
Example (using the example textfile above. file_1 is input and file_2 is
output)

read-input-file section.
  evaluate true
     when prog1K-filename_1 = "file_1"
        read file_1
     when prog1K-filename_1 = "file_4"
        read file_4
     when prog1K-filename_1 = "file_7"
        read file_7
     when other
        perform abend
  end-evaluate.

write-output-file section.
  evaluate true
     when prog1K-filename_2 = "file_2"
        write file_1
     when prog1K-filename_2 = "file_10"
        write file_10
     when other
        perform abend
  end-evaluate.


but this is coding that i dislike.



Sven Schneider
BEKO Informatik GmbH

http://www.dejanews.com/       Search, Read, Discuss, or Start Your Own
```

## Thread: Getting System date in 4 digit year format — 3 msgs

_Topics: [`Y2K and Year-2000 remediation`](topics.md#y2k) · [`Date and calendar processing`](topics.md#dates)_

### Getting System date in 4 digit year format

- **From:** "Duane Dobesh" <dobesh@mindspring.com>
- **Date:** 1999-03-25T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7dd86q$k7o$1@camel18.mindspring.com>`

```
Can someone tell me what is the easiest way to get the system date in 4
digit year format.  I use MF COBOL.    ACCEPT ... from date seems to only
provide the year in YY format.

I apologize if this question has been asked and answers probably multiple
times before.

Thanks
```

#### ↳ Re: Getting System date in 4 digit year format

- **From:** redsky@ibm.net (Thane Hubbell)
- **Date:** 1999-03-25T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<36fa365c.83884638@news1.ibm.net>`
- **References:** `<7dd86q$k7o$1@camel18.mindspring.com>`

```
On Thu, 25 Mar 1999 06:49:34 -0000, "Duane Dobesh"
<dobesh@mindspring.com> wrote:

>Can someone tell me what is the easiest way to get the system date in 4
>digit year format.  I use MF COBOL.    ACCEPT ... from date seems to only
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
>times before.

The "Standard" COBOL method is to use:

Move Function Current-Date to Date-Field.

01  Date-Field.
     03  Date-CC Pic 99.
     03  Date-YY Pic 99.
     03  Date-MM Pic 99.
     03  Date-DD Pic 99.
     03  Time-HH Pic 99.
     03  Time-MM Pic 99.
     03  Time-SS Pic 99.
     03  Time-hh Pic 99.
     03  GMT-Offset-Direction Pic X.
* GMT-Offset-Direction is + or -
     03  GMT-Offset Pic 9(4).

With MicroFocus you can also try ACCEPT The-Date from Date YYYYMMDD.

This will work if your version is recent enough.
```

#### ↳ Re: Getting System date in 4 digit year format

- **From:** "Calle" <markus.carl@fen.baynet.de>
- **Date:** 1999-03-25T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7de4jr$8j7$1@freenet-b.fen.baynet.de>`
- **References:** `<7dd86q$k7o$1@camel18.mindspring.com>`

```
Hi !
Try this :

WS:
       01  WS-DATUM-2000.
           03  DATUM-2000      PIC X(21).
           03  DAT-2000-R REDEFINES DATUM-2000.
           05  DAT2000         PIC 9(8).
           05  DAT2000-R REDEFINES DAT2000.
              07  D2-JJ         PIC 9(4).
              07  D2-MM         PIC 99.
              07  D2-TT         PIC 99.
           05  ZEIT2000.
             07  D2-STD         PIC 99.
             07  D2-MIN         PIC 99.
             07  D2-SEK         PIC 99.
           05  D2-HSEK          PIC 99.
           05  FILLER          PIC X(5).

Procedure :
              MOVE FUNCTION CURRENT-DATE TO DATUM-2000

The last FILLER is the difference from Greenwich-Mean-Time.

(MF-Cobol)

I hope I this will help you,

Calle


Duane Dobesh schrieb in Nachricht <7dd86q$k7o$1@camel18.mindspring.com>...
>Can someone tell me what is the easiest way to get the system date in 4
>digit year format.  I use MF COBOL.    ACCEPT ... from date seems to only
…[9 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

- **From:** smDelta@t-online.de (Martin Herbst)
- **Date:** 1999-03-25T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7dd6n7$h33$1@news04.btx.dtag.de>`

```
I have created a COBOL subprogram as shared library with the Fujitsu COBOL
V3 compiler on Sun Solaris. I now want to call this program from a C main
program. I have added the calls to JMPCINT2 and JMPCINT3 to my C program
like it is described in the User's Guide. But if I try to link the C program
I get error messages because auf undefined symbols JMPCINT2 and JMPCINT3.
In the User's Guide I can't find any information about the necessary
libraries or objects I have to link to my C program.

I hope somebody here in this newgroup can give me some help.

Martin Herbst
```

- **From:** "Donald Tees" <donald@willmack.com>

```
The most common cause of problems with this sort of thing is case
sensitivity.  I know how to fix it going the other way (Cobol calling C),
but have never done the C calling Cobol.  That may be the problem, though.

<a id="t-5ddd1d9bbc"></a>
## Thread: 3rd RFD: comp.soft-sys.acucorp — 1 this month / 4 total

_Thread started [1999-01](1999-01.md#t-5ddd1d9bbc)._

#### ↳ CFV: comp.soft-sys.acucorp

- **From:** Neil Crellin <neilc@wallaby.cc>
- **Date:** 1999-03-25T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** news.announce.newgroups,news.groups,alt.cobol,comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<922401846.23510@isc.org>`
- **References:** `<907917797.22206@isc.org> <915475698.556@isc.org> <917822140.20230@isc.org>`

```
                     FIRST CALL FOR VOTES (of 2)
               unmoderated group comp.soft-sys.acucorp

This CFV is to be distributed only by the votetaker.  It is not to be
posted to newsgroups, or mailed to mailing lists or individuals, except by
the votetaker.  Ballots or CFVs provided by anyone else will be invalid.

Newsgroups line:
comp.soft-sys.acucorp	All aspects of the use of Acucobol.

Votes must be received by 23:59:59 UTC, 15 Apr 1999.

This vote is being conducted by a neutral third party.  Questions
about the proposed group should be directed to the proponent.

Proponent: Scott Woodward <scottw@southpacfinance.com.au>
Votetaker: Neil Crellin <neilc@wallaby.cc>

RATIONALE: comp.soft-sys.acucorp

Acucobol is a company based in San Diego CA. Currently all
users and developers use email to communicate ideas and
problems to Acucobol (now known as Acucorp). These ideas
and problems however are not released to the general populous
of Acucobol users. This presents a problem for both Acucobol
and the Users.

The purpose of creating this comp.soft-sys.acucorp newsgroup
is to have a central point of contact for all users. Discussions
involving Acucobol-specific information would be greatly enhanced
by this group due to the world wide use of Acucobol.

Due to the specific nature of some of the Acucobol products, there
is a need for a separate group to discuss these topics as opposed to
just the Cobol language.

Here are some reasons in favor of a comp.soft-sys.acucorp:

Currently, most developers cannot easily come in contact with
other developers other than by attending Acucorp's yearly developer's
conference.

Acucobol contains significant extensions to the COBOL standard.
Discussion of these extensions would be of little interest to
comp.lang.cobol readers who are not Acucobol developers.

There are a number of Acucorp products which are used in conjunction
with Acucobol that do not directly involve COBOL. Because these
products tend to be used in larger, more complex systems they also tend
to cause more discussion than the base language. Such discussion would
be only marginally on topic in comp.lang.cobol.

CHARTER: comp.soft-sys.acucorp

This newsgroup is for discussion of all aspects of the use of
Acucobol.  Some examples of relevant topics are:

  Coding techniques
  Learning and teaching materials
  Discussions on coding
  User Support
  Technical Support
  User Discussions
  General Information

The primary purpose of this group is to pass information between the
users of Acucobol. Explicitly discouraged are the posting of commercial
advertisements, any kind of flaming and personal arguments, as well as
the posting of binaries of any kind.

END CHARTER.

IMPORTANT VOTING PROCEDURE NOTES: READ THIS BEFORE VOTING

The purpose of a Usenet vote is to determine the genuine interest in
reading the proposed newsgroup, and soliciting votes from uninterested
parties defeats this purpose.  Do *not* distribute this CFV;  instead,
direct people to the official CFV as posted to news.announce.newgroups.
Distributing specific voting instructions or pre-marked copies of
this CFV is considered vote fraud.

This is a public vote:  All email addresses, names and votes will be
listed in the final RESULT post.  The name used may be either a real
name or an established Usenet handle.

At most one vote is allowed per person or per account.  Duplicate
votes will be resolved in favor of the most recent valid vote.

Voters must mail their ballots directly to the votetaker.  Anonymous,
forwarded, or proxy votes are not valid, nor are votes mailed from
WWW/HTML/CGI forms (which should not exist).  Votes from nonexistent
accounts are also invalid, and the votetaker will reject any "munged"
address he cannot decipher immediately.

Please direct any questions to the votetaker at <neilc@wallaby.cc>

HOW TO VOTE:

Extract the ballot from the CFV by deleting everything before and after
the "BEGINNING OF BALLOT" and "END OF BALLOT" lines.  Don't worry about
the spacing of the columns or any quote characters (">") that your
reply inserts.  Please, DO NOT send the entire CFV back to me!

Fill in the ballot as shown below.  Please provide your REAL NAME and
indicate your desired vote in the appropriate locations inside the ballot.

Examples of how to properly indicate your vote:

  [ YES     ]  example.yes.vote
  [ NO      ]  example.no.vote
  [ ABSTAIN ]  example.abstention
  [ CANCEL  ]  example.cancellation

DO NOT modify, alter or delete any information in this ballot!
If you do, the voting software will probably reject your ballot.

When finished, MAIL the ballot to: < voting@uvv.wallaby.cc >
Just "replying" to this message should work, but check the "To:" line.

If you do not receive an acknowledgment of your vote within three
days contact the votetaker about the problem.  You are responsible
for reading your ack and making sure your vote is registered correctly.

If these instructions are unclear, please consult the Introduction to
Usenet Voting or the Usenet Voting FAQ at http://www.stanford.edu/~neilc/uvv.

## Thread: PowerCobol Help — 8 msgs

### PowerCobol Help

- **From:** "Scott Thomas" <sc.thos@mindonespring.com>
- **Date:** 1999-03-26T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7dhjbv$lre$1@camel29.mindspring.com>`

```
I seem unable to get timely responses or even responses from the folks at
Fujitsu so I though I would try here.  I have a couple of question I need
some help answering.

1)  Can you use a copy statement in PowerCobol?  How, and where is it
documented in the help/manuals?  When I try it blows up PowerCobol!

2)  Can I use CRB_Routines in PowerCobol?  Again where is is documented?  I
have not tried this yet just wanted to see if anyone else has tried this.

3)  Where can I get the Images that Fujitsu uses for the "DisplayMessage"
Method?  The question mark, exclaimation mark etc.

4)  And a general questions.  Are you having problems getting support from
Fujitsu?

Thanks in advance for your help.

My e-mail is not as noted.  If you want to send an email instead of a post
it is my firstname.lastname@mindspring.com.
```

#### ↳ Re: PowerCobol Help

- **From:** "Donald Tees" <donald@willmack.com>
- **Date:** 1999-03-27T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7dig5t$l1a$1@news.igs.net>`
- **References:** `<7dhjbv$lre$1@camel29.mindspring.com>`

```
Scott Thomas wrote in message

>1)  Can you use a copy statement in PowerCobol?  How, and where is it
>documented in the help/manuals?  When I try it blows up PowerCobol!


Yes.  The file is put in quotes. IE
          copy "\library\subroutines\abc.cbl".

PowerCobol seems to use a pre-processor for its object reconciliation, so
you cannot include invocations in the copy, or they will bomb.  I've never
had any trouble with standard record and procedure copies though.

>
>2)  Can I use CRB_Routines in PowerCobol?  Again where is is documented?  I
>have not tried this yet just wanted to see if anyone else has tried this.


Yes, I've had no problems with it.
>
>3)  Where can I get the Images that Fujitsu uses for the "DisplayMessage"
>Method?  The question mark, exclaimation mark etc.
>

Don't have a clue.
>
4)  And a general questions.  Are you having problems getting support from
>Fujitsu?


I did not pay for it, so have not expected any. I have been correct so far.
```

```

Scott Thomas wrote in message <7dhjbv$lre$1@camel29.mindspring.com>...
>
>1)  Can you use a copy statement in PowerCobol?  How, and where is it
>documented in the help/manuals?  When I try it blows up PowerCobol!

## Here's a trick. Right click on [Script][COBOL Script], then "Properties"
There you will find a place to supply the path for your copybook libraries.
The path can be specific ("C:\SOURCE\COPY"), relative (..\COPY), or
by device name (\\SERVER\SERV-C\SOURCE\COPY). When the path
is implicitly supplied, you can use statements of the form:

        01  COPY  MYSTUFF.
>
>2)  Can I use CRB_Routines in PowerCobol?  Again where is is documented?  I
>have not tried this yet just wanted to see if anyone else has tried this.

I think they may have the complete document on their web site. If you mean
the
CBL_OPEN_FILE,
CBL_XOR,
CBL_GET_MOUSE_MASK
stuff.
>
>3)  Where can I get the Images that Fujitsu uses for the "DisplayMessage"
>Method?  The question mark, exclaimation mark etc.

## Don't know that one.
>
>4)  And a general questions.  Are you having problems getting support from
>Fujitsu?

## Not really. We send e-mail - sometimes they answer. Talking to a person
is
tougher inasmuch as they don't have in-house advisors. We have to get called
back.
>
>Thanks in advance for your help.
>
>My e-mail is not as noted.  If you want to send an email instead of a post
>it is my firstname.lastname@mindspring.com.


## I love your email address. Almost as good as yournamehere@mindspring.com
```

## Thread: help Looking for some code to do a Bubble sort in Cobol/Fortran — 6 msgs

### help Looking for some code to do a Bubble sort in Cobol/Fortran

- **From:** "spacesspaces" <spacesspaces@email.msn.com>
- **Date:** 1999-03-26T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol,fj.comp.lang.cobol,fj.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<eQ3QQm4d#GA.333@upnetnews02.moswest.msn.net>`

```
help       Looking for some code to do a "Bubble" sort in Cobol/Fortran


have a need to sort some internal tables, multikey.


thanks in advance
ed
.
.
.
Got Lotto ?
Brighton Computer Group
http://www.geocities.com/Eureka/Boardroom/7821/lgames.htm
.
.
```

#### ↳ Re: help Looking for some code to do a Bubble sort in Cobol/Fortran

- **From:** twymer@aol.com (Twymer)
- **Date:** 1999-03-26T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<19990326083844.23496.00000484@ng-ch1.aol.com>`
- **References:** `<eQ3QQm4d#GA.333@upnetnews02.moswest.msn.net>`

```
here is some pseudocode:

set table pointers to 1 and 2.
set the "switched" switch to no.
compare the first 2 keys.
Is the first key less than the second key?
If yes increment both pointers by 1
else
   move the second key into a temp area
   move the first key into the second key
   move the temp area into the first key
   set the "switched" to yes
   increment both pointers by 1
endif

If the second table pointer points to end of table,  and the "switched" switch
is Yes...reset the switch to No and set the table pointers to 1 and 2. 

keep reading the table until the second table pointer points to end of table
and the "switched" switch is no

Good luck.

TW
```

#### ↳ Re: help Looking for some code to do a Bubble sort in Cobol/Fortran

- **From:** "Jay Siegel" <ka2csh@nospam.nowhere>
- **Date:** 1999-03-26T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol,fj.comp.lang.cobol,fj.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<Q1.D1.412IAo9yMtq4soqs4.NR@nospam.nowhere>`
- **References:** `<eQ3QQm4d#GA.333@upnetnews02.moswest.msn.net>`

```
"space" == "spacesspaces" writes:

space> help       Looking for some code to do a "Bubble" sort in
space> Cobol/Fortran
space> 
space> 
space> have a need to sort some internal tables, multikey.
space> 
space> 
space> thanks in advance
space> ed
space> .

    Bubble sort?  I'd recommend at least a Shell sort algorythm,
    while the Fast sort algorythm is even faster.  The Bubble sort
    algorythm is one of the slowest algorythms for sorting.

Jay
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: help Looking for some code to do a Bubble sort in Cobol/Fortran

- **From:** Howard Brazee <brazee@NOSPAMhome.com>
- **Date:** 1999-03-29T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol,fj.comp.lang.cobol,fj.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<36FF8FFC.FE03B90D@NOSPAMhome.com>`
- **References:** `<eQ3QQm4d#GA.333@upnetnews02.moswest.msn.net> <Q1.D1.412IAo9yMtq4soqs4.NR@nospam.nowhere>`

```
Jay Siegel wrote:
> 
> "space" == "spacesspaces" writes:
…[16 more quoted lines elided]…
> Jay

True.  But I am guessing about why a COBOL program needs to code a sort
at all.  I suspect it is for a very small amount of data, maybe to order
some data on a screen.  In that case, who cares which sort is faster. 
Just code which is easier (to maintain and avoid bugs).

Otherwise use a sort utility (external sort, COBOL sort, or SQL ordered
input).
```

#### ↳ Re: Need COBOL650 Linker program.. shareware

- **From:** "Kevin G. Rhoads" <T_Rhoads@NoSpam.CLASSIC.MSN.COM>
- **Date:** 1999-03-27T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<01be7882$2c4edcc0$LocalHost@stupidwin95>`
- **References:** `<36FB9BA9.EF523BA5@Keane.com>`

```
There are a number of Linkers available as shareware or 
freeware.  ALINK (for which source is available) is under
active development presently.  Also the old Digital Research
tools are being made available (CBASIC, PL/I &c) freely
for non-commercial purposes -- the CP/M-86 and the DOS
versions and they come with linkers.  The ValArrow package
has a linker.  Plus some of the last 16 bit linkers from MS are
(or at least were) available for download from the MS website.

I'd suggest you start with Simtel, you'll find plenty.
```

<a id="t-b3095d7764"></a>
## Thread: Response to comp.soft-sys.acucorp - Call for votes — 5 msgs

### Response to comp.soft-sys.acucorp - Call for votes

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 1999-03-27T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol,alt.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7di9cn$74s@dfw-ixnews6.ix.netcom.com>`

```
FYI, the following is what I submitted concerning the call for votes on
creating the new acucorp newsgroup,

  ***

"In the past I have expressed my belief that this was a mistake.  I still
vote NO - although I believe that this proposal is much better than any
previous "version" of the proposal.

HOWEVER, there is one statement within this document that is simply so FALSE
that I think it needs to be addressed specifically.  The document states,

"Acucobol contains significant extensions to the COBOL standard.
 Discussion of these extensions would be of little interest to
 comp.lang.cobol readers who are not Acucobol developers."

The proposer of this group has been repeatedly told that this statement
simply is not true.  There is ABSOLUTELY no evidence within the history of
comp.lang.cobol that there is anything other than SIGNIFICANT interest among
most readers and contributors in the extensions available in the AcuCOBOL
product - and all other COBOL implementations.  Furthermore, it is my belief
that this MISconception is what has caused this group to be proposed and
that even when it comes to the non-COBOL products and features of Acucorp,
these could be well processed and discussed in the existing newsgroup

Again, I vote NO - and really hope that - should this group be formed - that
the misconception of comp.lang.cobol and its participants be corrected as
quickly and thoroughly as possible."
```

stuff from this newsgroup.
```
