## Thread: Help with Powercobol 4.0 with ODBC — 1 msgs

_Topics: [`Databases and SQL`](topics.md#databases) · [`Help requests and how-to`](topics.md#help)_

### Help with Powercobol 4.0 with ODBC

- **From:** derichs@casema.net (Peter)
- **Date:** 1999-03-31T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<MPG.116c48ca3a7bfe69989680@News.casema.net>`

```
I'm trying to get some large application ported from Micro Focus Cobol to 
Fujitsu Powercobol. Everything works well -apart from some minor bugs-.
But connecting to an ODBC-database I keep running into the same error(s).
When I create (type it in) an ODBC-database using MS-Access, the programs 
connect to the database without problems. When a database is converted 
with DataJunction6.5 (via ODBC) I get the message: Expected more 
parameters (35). The database has 31 fields. The sql.log gives the same 
message.

Has anyone an idea???
Thanks in advance.

Peter Derichs
derichs@casema.net.
```

---

<a id="t-bcdcac0867"></a>
## Thread: Help with Powercobol 4.0 with ODBC — 1 msgs

_Topics: [`Databases and SQL`](topics.md#databases) · [`Help requests and how-to`](topics.md#help)_

### Help with Powercobol 4.0 with ODBC

- **From:** derichs@casema.net (Peter)
- **Date:** 1999-03-31T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<MPG.116c49b4df047cb6989681@News.casema.net>`

```

I'm trying to get some large application ported from Micro Focus Cobol to 
Fujitsu Powercobol. Everything works well -apart from some minor bugs-.
But connecting to an ODBC-database I keep running into the same error(s).
When I create (type it in) an ODBC-database using MS-Access, the programs 
connect to the database without problems. When a database is converted 
with DataJunction6.5 (via ODBC) I get the message: Expected more 
parameters (35). The database has 31 fields. The sql.log gives the same 
message.

Is there a difference between files created with MS-Access and files 
created via ODBC??

Has anyone an idea???
Thanks in advance.

Peter Derichs
derichs@casema.net.
```

## Thread: Is Microfocus the only PC based version of COBOL available ? — 4 this month / 8 total

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

### Is Microfocus the only PC based version of COBOL available ?

- **From:** "Thomas Stanley" <tommy@project23.freeserve.co.uk>
- **Date:** 1999-03-31T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7dtv6f$pvi$1@news7.svr.pol.co.uk>`

```
Having recently started programming Cobol in a mainframe environment, I'd
desperately like to improve my 'hands on skills' as quickly as I can.
Unfortunately this would mean staying at work for 20 hrs a day. Not very
practical !!

I therefore need something to run on my PC at home that is going to create
that environment as best as possible with regards to JCL, compiling, linking
etc.

A few people have pointed me in the direction of Microfocus Cobol.
Could any of you experts be able to point me in the right direction and give
me a little advice

Regards (in advance !)
```

#### ↳ Re: Is Microfocus the only PC based version of COBOL available ?

- **From:** "J. Mark Holder" <holdm@interpath.com>
- **Date:** 1999-03-31T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3702E439.F3A6DE05@interpath.com>`
- **References:** `<7dtv6f$pvi$1@news7.svr.pol.co.uk>`

```
I've used MFCobol since it first came out on a 5.25 floppy in the mid 80's. It's
an excellent product. The cost has risen - just purchased 4 workbench 4.x copies
for $12k (ouch) (OS/2,WinNT,Win95 and DOS). Used their CICS product also, but
prefer IBM's due to better administration functionality.
The debugger is one of my favorite tools. Bottomline - have used on major
development projects for the Mainframe platform and if you define your
environment right on the PC, your code migrates without modification! Also have
delivered Cobol PC apps.

Yes, I'm biased towards MicroFocus because 1) it has worked consistantly and 2)
due to reason 1 have not used other flavors of PC Cobol. Why change when my
requirements are met? Not blind, just content.

Mark

Thomas Stanley wrote:

> Having recently started programming Cobol in a mainframe environment, I'd
> desperately like to improve my 'hands on skills' as quickly as I can.
…[11 more quoted lines elided]…
> Regards (in advance !)
```

<a id="t-41adcc34c0"></a>
## Thread: Microfocus Cobol v1.1 — 2 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

### Microfocus Cobol v1.1

- **From:** mkrei@my-dejanews.com
- **Date:** 1999-03-31T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7druki$le6$1@nnrp1.dejanews.com>`

```
I have a situation where I have an old legacy Microfocus Cobol version 1.1
program.  It is running on a Xenix operating system and I think the version of
Xenix is 2.2.3(?).  I would like to port this system over to a Windows NT 4.0
operating system.  Can anyone tell me how I would go about doing this?  Is
there a specific compiler I need for Windows NT or can I convert the old
version into another version which would run on the NT machine.  I appreciate
any help and thank you in advance.

-----------== Posted via Deja News, The Discussion Network ==----------
http://www.dejanews.com/       Search, Read, Discuss, or Start Your Own
```

#### ↳ Re: Microfocus Cobol v1.1

- **From:** redsky@ibm.net (Thane Hubbell)
- **Date:** 1999-03-31T00:00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3701ad49.193023418@news1.ibm.net>`
- **References:** `<7druki$le6$1@nnrp1.dejanews.com>`

```
On Wed, 31 Mar 1999 01:43:48 GMT, mkrei@my-dejanews.com wrote:

>I have a situation where I have an old legacy Microfocus Cobol version 1.1
>program.  It is running on a Xenix operating system and I think the version of
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
>any help and thank you in advance.

The files may be a bigger issue than the code.  You should be able to
take your code and compile it with the latest MicroFocus (Merant)
compiler without change.  If you change compiler vendors, any MF
specific things you used (and it's easy to do!) MAY not function.
```
